title: Pesto Panko Chicken with Green Beans & Roasted Potatoes
description: HelloFresh's #1 Hall of Fame recipe — tender chicken breasts spread with basil pesto, coated in a crispy panko and mozzarella crust, baked to golden perfection and served with roasted potatoes and green beans.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Line two baking sheets with foil and coat with cooking spray.
Cut potatoes into 1-inch cubes. Toss with 1½ tbsp olive oil, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Spread on one baking sheet. Roast for 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, pat chicken breasts dry. Place on the second baking sheet. Season lightly with salt and pepper.
In a small bowl, mix panko breadcrumbs and shredded mozzarella together.
Spread 1 tablespoon of pesto over the top of each chicken breast. Press the panko-mozzarella mixture firmly onto the pesto to adhere.
After the potatoes have roasted 15 minutes, push them to one side of their pan and add the green beans tossed with the remaining 1½ tbsp olive oil and Italian seasoning.
Place the chicken in the oven alongside the vegetables. Roast everything for 18–22 minutes, until the chicken reaches 165°F and the panko crust is golden and crispy.
Squeeze fresh lemon juice over the chicken and vegetables before serving.
tips: Pound chicken breasts to an even thickness (about ¾ inch) for more even cooking.
Use store-bought basil pesto for convenience, or make your own with basil, pine nuts, parmesan, garlic, and olive oil.
For extra crispiness, broil the chicken for the last 2 minutes — watch closely so the panko doesn't burn.
Leftovers reheat well in a 350°F oven for 10 minutes to keep the crust crispy.
